choices.label: 물고기 먹기 (체력 전부 회복)
choices.notes: 전체 회복은 매우 가치 있음, 특히 중반에. 덱에 추가되는 저주는 작은 대가.

conditions.notes: 회복이 필요 없음 — 다른 선택지를 고려.
얇은 덱에서 저주가 더 아픔 더 자주 뽑게 되므로.

choices.label: 물고기에게 먹이주기 (최대 HP 증가)
choices.notes: 최대 HP는 장기 투자. Act 1에서 더 좋음 — 전체 런에 걸쳐 이득.

conditions.notes: 초반 최대 HP는 전체 런에 걸쳐 이득.
런 후반이라 최대 HP 가치 낮음.

choices.label: 무시하기
choices.notes: 다른 선택지도 의미 있는 가치 제공. 단점을 감수할 수 없을 때만 스킵.

choices.label: 우상 가져가기 (골드 획득, 함정 발동)
choices.notes: The gold is substantial and helps you buy key cards or relics at the next shop.

conditions.notes: The trap damage can kill you or leave you dangerously low.
You can absorb the trap damage comfortably.

choices.label: 우상 남겨두기
choices.notes: Safe but you miss out on significant gold. Only worth it if you can't afford HP loss.

conditions.notes: Preserving HP is more important than gold when you're low.

choices.label: 축복 선택 (다양한 보너스)
choices.notes: Neow's blessings set the tone for your run. Prioritize relic or card removal options when available.

choices.label: 랜덤 희귀 유물로 교환
choices.notes: High variance — you might get something amazing or something useless. Worth it if the downside is manageable.

choices.label: 시작 유물 교체
choices.notes: Boss relics are powerful but you lose your starter synergy. Usually worth the gamble on higher difficulties.

choices.label: 카드 업그레이드
choices.notes: A free upgrade is always solid. Prioritize upgrading key scaling or damage cards.

conditions.notes: Upgrades are more impactful when your deck is lean.

choices.label: 카드 제거
choices.notes: Card removal thins your deck and improves consistency. Remove Strikes first, then Defends.

conditions.notes: Bloated decks benefit enormously from removal.
Your deck is already thin — an upgrade may be better.

choices.label: 책 읽기 (카드 선택)
choices.notes: A curated card selection is excellent for finding cards that fit your archetype.

conditions.notes: Be selective — only take a card if it genuinely improves your deck.

choices.label: 수면 (HP 회복)
choices.notes: The heal is decent but you miss the card selection opportunity.

conditions.notes: If you're dangerously low, surviving takes priority over card quality.

choices.label: 수락
choices.notes: Bites provide sustain through lifesteal but you lose all Strikes. Strong if your deck doesn't rely on Strike synergies.

conditions.notes: In a thin deck, Bites become a reliable healing engine.
With many cards, you draw Bites less often so the sustain is diluted.

choices.label: 거절
choices.notes: Safe option. Keep your Strikes if you have Strike synergy relics or cards.

choices.label: Rest (heal HP)
choices.notes: A solid heal when you need it. Simple and reliable.

conditions.notes: You don't need healing — consider other options if available.

choices.label: Stoke the fire (upgrade)
choices.notes: Upgrades have lasting value. Prefer this if your HP is comfortable.

conditions.notes: Healing may be more urgent than upgrading right now.

choices.label: Read the tome (gain powerful card or relic, plus a curse)
choices.notes: The reward can be strong but the curse pollutes your deck. Worth it if you have curse removal options.

conditions.notes: A curse in a thin deck is devastating to consistency.
The curse is diluted in a larger deck, making the trade more acceptable.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Safe play. Avoiding curses keeps your deck clean.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: The relic is usually worth the HP cost. Relics provide permanent value.

conditions.notes: You can't afford the HP loss when you're already hurting.
Plenty of HP to spare — take the relic.

choices.label: Decline
choices.notes: Only if you can't afford the HP hit. Missing a relic opportunity is a real cost.

choices.label: 카드 업그레이드
choices.notes: Free upgrades are always welcome. Pick your most impactful card to upgrade.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Gold is useful but upgrades tend to provide more lasting value.

conditions.notes: If you're broke with a shop coming, the gold can be critical.

choices.label: Give gold (receive a reward)
choices.notes: The reward varies. Worth it if you have gold to spare.

conditions.notes: Don't spend gold you can't afford.
You have plenty of gold — invest it here for a bonus.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Taking gold is the aggressive play. Good if you need shop money.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Both other options provide value. Only leave if the costs are too steep.

choices.label: 랜덤 유물 획득
choices.notes: Relics are powerful permanent upgrades, but losing max HP weakens your survivability for the rest of the run.

conditions.notes: You have HP to spare — the relic is likely worth the trade.
Losing max HP when you're already low is extremely dangerous.
Early max HP loss hurts for the whole run, but an early relic compounds in value too.
Less run remaining to suffer from reduced max HP. Take the relic.

choices.label: 거절
choices.notes: Preserves your max HP. The safe play when you can't afford to get any squishier.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Gold fuels your shop purchases. A solid, low-risk reward.

conditions.notes: You already have plenty of gold — the heal might be better.

choices.label: Touch the sphere (heal HP)
choices.notes: Healing is always useful. Choose this over gold when your HP is hurting.

conditions.notes: Healing takes priority when you're in danger.
You're healthy — take the gold instead.

choices.label: Buy potions at discount
choices.notes: Potions can swing tough fights. Worth buying if you have open potion slots and upcoming elites or bosses.

conditions.notes: Even at a discount, you might need that gold for more impactful shop purchases.
Stock up — potions give you insurance for hard fights.

choices.label: Decline
choices.notes: Skip if your potion slots are full or you're saving gold for a key shop purchase.

choices.label: 카드 업그레이드
choices.notes: A free upgrade with no downside. Always take this unless you have nothing worth upgrading.

choices.label: Decline
choices.notes: There's no reason to skip a free upgrade.

choices.label: Cross carefully (small safe reward)
choices.notes: The safe option. Guaranteed but modest reward. Good when you can't afford to lose HP.

conditions.notes: Don't risk falling when you're already hurting.

choices.label: Rush across (risk falling for gold or relic)
choices.notes: The high-reward option. The potential relic or large gold payout is usually worth the risk.

conditions.notes: Falling could end your run. Play it safe.
You can absorb a fall. Go for the big reward.

choices.label: Turn back
choices.notes: You gain nothing. Almost always worse than crossing carefully.

choices.label: 수락
choices.notes: The buff is permanent but so is the downside. Evaluate whether the upside outweighs the cost for your build.

conditions.notes: More time to benefit from the buff. The downside is manageable early.
Less time to benefit, so the downside matters more relative to the upside.

choices.label: Reject the symbiote
choices.notes: Clean and safe. If your deck is already working well, don't introduce a wildcard.

choices.label: Invest in potion research (gain potions)
choices.notes: Free potions are strong. Potions can carry you through elite fights and bosses.

conditions.notes: If there's a gold cost, be cautious when you're broke.

choices.label: Drink experimental potion (random effect)
choices.notes: High variance. Could be great or terrible. Only gamble if you're in a position to absorb a bad outcome.

choices.label: Decline
choices.notes: Missing out on free or cheap potions is rarely correct.

choices.label: Explore the treasure (gain relic or gold)
choices.notes: Treasure events are high-value. The reward is almost always worth pursuing.

conditions.notes: If exploring costs HP, be careful when you're low.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Walking away from treasure is hard to justify unless the risk is lethal.

choices.label: Choose option A
choices.notes: Compare both options carefully. Pick whichever one fills a gap in your current build.

choices.label: Choose option B
choices.notes: Evaluate based on what your deck needs right now. There's no universally better pick.

choices.label: Inhale deeply (random positive and negative effects)
choices.notes: High variance — could grant great buffs or painful debuffs. Take the gamble when your run feels average.

conditions.notes: Random damage effects could end your run.
You can survive bad outcomes. Roll the dice.

choices.label: Hold your breath (small safe reward or nothing)
choices.notes: The conservative play. Good when your run is already strong and you don't want to disrupt it.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Getting nothing from an event is almost always the worst outcome.

choices.label: 전투
choices.notes: The rewards from the fight are usually worth it. Nest fights tend to be manageable in Act 1.

conditions.notes: You can't afford combat damage when you're already hurting.

choices.label: 유물 획득
choices.notes: A reward without combat is efficient. Great when you want to conserve HP for upcoming fights.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Both other options are better. Only leave if you're critically low on HP.

choices.label: Cut through (event combat, then reward)
choices.notes: The combat is usually worth the reward. Treat it like a slightly easier elite encounter.

conditions.notes: Event combat when you're low is a run-ending risk.
You're healthy — fight for the reward.

choices.label: Go around (safe but slower)
choices.notes: You avoid damage but miss the reward. A fine choice when survival matters more than loot.

conditions.notes: Staying alive is the priority.

choices.label: Go left (path A rewards)
choices.notes: Maze paths offer different rewards. Pick based on what your run needs — cards, gold, or healing.

choices.label: Go right (path B rewards)
choices.notes: Compare the visible rewards of each path. Neither is universally better.

choices.label: Go straight (path C rewards)
choices.notes: A third route with its own reward profile. Choose based on your deck's current needs.

choices.label: 카드 변형
choices.notes: Transforming a weak card like Strike can yield a powerful replacement. High upside with manageable risk.

conditions.notes: Every card matters more in a small deck — transform your worst card for a potential upgrade.
You have plenty of filler to transform. Go for it.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Transforming is almost always better than walking away. The floor is a Strike and the ceiling is a rare.

choices.label: Eat the cheese (heal HP, possible trap)
choices.notes: The healing is significant. The trap risk is usually worth it for the HP recovery.

conditions.notes: 회복이 필요 없음 badly. The trap risk may not be worth it.
You need the healing. The risk is worth taking.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: The safe play. Pick this if you're in a comfortable HP range and don't want to gamble.

choices.label: 골드 지불
choices.notes: The delayed reward is usually worth the investment. The payoff compounds over the run.

conditions.notes: You can't afford to invest gold you need for immediate purchases.
More time for the seed to pay off. Plant early.

choices.label: Take the seed (immediate small reward)
choices.notes: The instant reward is smaller but guaranteed. Better when you need something right now.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Both other options give you something. Don't walk away empty-handed.

choices.label: Study the tablet (gain information or map reveal)
choices.notes: Information helps you plan your route but doesn't directly strengthen your deck.

conditions.notes: Early information is more valuable — you have more decisions ahead.

choices.label: Smash the tablet (gain immediate reward)
choices.notes: A tangible reward is usually better than information. Cards, gold, or relics directly improve your run.

choices.label: 유물 획득
choices.notes: Trading gold for a relic is almost always worthwhile. Relics provide permanent power.

conditions.notes: You can't afford the offering. Save your gold.
You have gold to spare. Invest in a permanent relic.

choices.label: Pray without offering (small buff or nothing)
choices.notes: Free but unreliable. A reasonable fallback if you're short on gold.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Even praying for free is better than walking away.

choices.label: Drink from the spring (heal HP)
choices.notes: Free healing with no downside. Take it when you're below full HP.

conditions.notes: You're already healthy. The upgrade might serve you better.

choices.label: 카드 업그레이드
choices.notes: A free upgrade is very strong. Choose this when your HP is comfortable.

conditions.notes: Consider healing first — you can find upgrades elsewhere.

choices.label: Listen to the whispers (gain a card or power)
choices.notes: The whispers often reveal powerful options. Risk is usually minimal.

conditions.notes: Be selective about adding cards to an already tight deck.

choices.label: Cover your ears and push through (safe, minor reward)
choices.notes: Safe but you miss potential power. Only worth it if your build is already coherent.

choices.label: Shout back (risky, high reward)
choices.notes: 고위험 고보상. The aggressive option when your run needs a boost.

conditions.notes: Too risky when you're already fragile.
You can handle the downside. Swing for the fences.

choices.label: Study the carvings (gain a card)
choices.notes: A curated card add is strong in Act 1 when you're building your deck's identity.

conditions.notes: Only take the card if it genuinely fits your strategy.
Building your deck early is critical. A good card here sets your direction.

choices.label: 카드 업그레이드
choices.notes: Free upgrades are always valuable. Great if you already have a card worth upgrading.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Gold is fine but the card or upgrade options usually provide more lasting value.

conditions.notes: If you need gold for a crucial shop purchase, take it.

choices.label: Soak in the baths (heal HP at a cost)
choices.notes: The healing is substantial. The cost is usually worth paying for the recovery.

conditions.notes: Don't pay the cost when you don't need the heal.
You desperately need the HP. Pay the price.

choices.label: Explore the baths (find items)
choices.notes: The items can be useful but the healing option is often more impactful.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Both other options provide value. Don't leave empty-handed.

choices.label: Enter the door of light (safer reward)
choices.notes: The light door offers a reliable, positive outcome. Good for stable runs.

conditions.notes: Play it safe when you're vulnerable.

choices.label: Enter the door of dark (riskier, bigger reward)
choices.notes: Higher ceiling but also a potential downside. Choose when your run needs a power spike.

conditions.notes: You can handle a bad outcome. Go for the bigger reward.
A bad dark door outcome could be fatal.

choices.label: Dive in (event combat for large gold reward)
choices.notes: The gold payout is large enough to justify the combat. Treat it as an investment.

conditions.notes: Combat when you're low is a run-ending gamble.
You can afford the fight. Dive in for the gold.
You already have plenty of gold — the risk may not be worth it.

choices.label: Grab what you can from the surface (small gold)
choices.notes: Some gold with no risk. The cautious option.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Even the surface grab is better than nothing.

choices.label: Touch the flower (heal a large amount)
choices.notes: Significant healing. One of the best heal events in the game.

conditions.notes: Less value when you're already near full. Consider the relic option.

choices.label: 유물 획득
choices.notes: A relic is permanent power. Choose this when your HP is healthy.

conditions.notes: The heal may be more critical to your survival right now.
You don't need healing — grab the relic.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Both other options are strong. Don't pass this up.

choices.label: Trade with the dolls (exchange a card for a better one)
choices.notes: Card trading lets you upgrade your deck quality without changing deck size. Trade away your worst card.

conditions.notes: Every card swap matters more in a thin deck.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: A smaller but safe reward. Good if you don't have a bad card worth trading.

choices.label: Play with the dolls (random outcome)
choices.notes: Random events can swing either way. Only pick this if you're feeling lucky.

conditions.notes: Random bad outcomes are too dangerous when you're fragile.

choices.label: Climb into a hole (explore for rewards)
choices.notes: The rewards for exploring are generally worth the minor risks.

conditions.notes: Be cautious — some holes may have hazards.

choices.label: Examine the holes from outside (safe observation)
choices.notes: Safer but with smaller returns. Good for cautious play.

choices.label: Walk past
choices.notes: Exploring is almost always better than ignoring the event entirely.

choices.label: 전투
choices.notes: The combat reward is substantial. The fight is manageable if your deck has reasonable damage output.

conditions.notes: Event combat is too risky at low HP.
You're healthy enough to fight. Go for the reward.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Skip the fight and take a smaller guaranteed reward. The practical choice.

conditions.notes: Take what you can safely. No need to risk a fight.

choices.label: 전투
choices.notes: Knight fights in Act 2 offer elite-tier rewards. Worth it if your deck can handle it.

conditions.notes: Don't pick a fight you might not survive.
You're in fighting shape. Claim the reward.

choices.label: Talk to the knight (gain information or small reward)
choices.notes: A peaceful resolution with a smaller payoff. The diplomat's choice.

conditions.notes: Talk your way out when you can't afford to fight.

choices.label: Ignore and move on
choices.notes: Even talking gives you something. Don't walk away.

choices.label: 카드 변형
choices.notes: Modifying your deck is almost always valuable. Transform weak cards or upgrade key ones.

conditions.notes: Deck modifications are extra impactful in thin decks.

choices.label: Look into the future (gain insight or a reward)
choices.notes: The future option varies. Evaluate what's offered compared to the past option.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Immediate rewards over long-term deck improvement. Good when you need gold for an upcoming shop.

conditions.notes: If a shop is coming, the gold could let you buy a key card or relic.

choices.label: Drink green tea (heal HP)
choices.notes: Reliable healing. The Tea Master is one of the better heal events.

conditions.notes: You don't need healing. Consider the other teas.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Gold is useful for shop purchases. Pick this when you're healthy but short on funds.

conditions.notes: You need the gold more than healing right now.
You already have plenty of gold.

choices.label: Drink special tea (unique effect)
choices.notes: The special tea has a unique effect that varies. Usually interesting but not always the optimal pick.

choices.label: Follow the wisp (explore for rewards)
choices.notes: Following the wisp leads to hidden rewards. The journey is usually worth the detour.

conditions.notes: The path might have hazards. Proceed with caution.

choices.label: Capture the wisp (gain a specific reward)
choices.notes: A guaranteed but smaller reward. Good when you want certainty over adventure.

choices.label: Let it go
choices.notes: The wisp offers free value. Don't ignore it.

choices.label: Play Wongo's game (gamble gold for rewards)
choices.notes: Gambling events are high variance. The expected value can be positive but the floor is losing your bet.

conditions.notes: You can afford to lose the bet. Play for the upside.
Don't gamble what you can't afford to lose.

choices.label: Bet big (higher stakes gambling)
choices.notes: Maximum risk, maximum reward. Only for runs where you need a miracle or have gold to burn.

conditions.notes: High reward potential but you could lose a lot.
You absolutely cannot afford this.

choices.label: Walk away from the table
choices.notes: The responsible choice. Protect your gold when you have important purchases planned.

choices.label: Weave a card into your deck (add a curated card)
choices.notes: The Zen Weaver offers strong, targeted card additions. Look for cards that complement your archetype.

conditions.notes: Only add a card if it truly strengthens your deck's game plan.
A good card won't hurt a larger deck and might fill a missing piece.

choices.label: 카드 제거
choices.notes: Card removal is premium. Thin your deck by removing Strikes or other dead weight.

conditions.notes: Your deck needs trimming. Remove the weakest card.
Your deck is already lean. Only remove if something is actively bad.

choices.label: Meditate (gain a temporary buff)
choices.notes: Temporary buffs are less valuable than permanent deck changes. Usually the weakest option here.

choices.label: Browse the wares (triggers event combat)
choices.notes: This 'shop' is actually a trap fight. Be ready for combat when you interact. The reward after winning is good.

conditions.notes: Walking into a surprise fight at low HP is extremely dangerous.
You can handle the ambush. Win the fight, claim the reward.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: If you recognize the trap, leaving saves you from an unwanted fight. Smart when you're fragile.

conditions.notes: Trust your instincts and get out safely.

choices.label: Pay respects (gain a blessing or positive effect)
choices.notes: Respectful choices tend to give clean, positive rewards without downsides. The honorable path pays off.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: The loot is tempting but the curse can hurt. Worth it if you have curse removal or a large deck to dilute it.

conditions.notes: A curse in a thin deck is crippling.
The curse is diluted in a bigger deck. Take the treasure.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: Safe but you miss the reward. Only if neither option suits your situation.

choices.label: Eat the red mushroom (heal HP)
choices.notes: Red mushrooms provide reliable healing. A safe pick when your HP is low.

conditions.notes: You don't need healing. Try a different mushroom.
Eat up and recover.

choices.label: Eat the blue mushroom (gain a buff or card)
choices.notes: The blue mushroom offers power upgrades. Good when your HP is fine and you want to grow stronger.

conditions.notes: You're healthy — invest in power instead of healing.

choices.label: Eat the strange mushroom (random powerful effect)
choices.notes: High variance. Could be amazing or painful. The gambler's mushroom.

conditions.notes: A bad random effect could end your run.
You can survive a bad roll, but the blue mushroom might be more reliable.

choices.label: Ask for wisdom (gain a card selection)
choices.notes: Ranwid offers powerful Act 3 cards. Late-game card additions should be strong scaling or finishers.

conditions.notes: Only take a card if it's genuinely excellent for your build.

choices.label: Ask for strength (gain a permanent buff)
choices.notes: Permanent buffs in Act 3 help you push through the final boss. Solid and reliable.

choices.label: 카드 제거
choices.notes: Healing plus card removal is an excellent combo. Great for cleaning up your deck before the boss.

conditions.notes: Healing before the final boss is critical.
Remove a weak card to improve your deck's consistency for the boss.

choices.label: Trade a relic (swap one relic for another)
choices.notes: Evaluate carefully. Trade away relics that don't fit your build for ones that might. High variance.

conditions.notes: By Act 3 you know which relics are dead weight. Trade them for potential upgrades.

choices.label: Decline the trade
choices.notes: If all your relics are useful, don't gamble them away. Know when to hold.

choices.label: Join the party (participate for social rewards)
choices.notes: Joining the tea party typically offers healing and a card or relic. A pleasant event with good outcomes.

choices.label: Ask for a special blend (unique reward)
choices.notes: The special blend has a unique effect. Can be very strong but less predictable than just joining.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: Risky but profitable. The consequences vary — could be a curse, combat, or just bad reputation.

conditions.notes: Tempting when broke, but the consequences might cost more than you gain.
If consequences involve combat, at least you can handle it.

choices.label: 떠나기
choices.notes: The party has gifts for you. Leaving empty-handed is wasteful.

choices.label: 수락
choices.notes: Act 3 trial combat is tough but the rewards are excellent. A test of your deck's readiness for the boss.

conditions.notes: You can't afford a hard fight right before the boss.
You're ready. Prove your worth and claim the reward.

choices.label: Decline the trial
choices.notes: Preserving HP for the boss is sometimes more important than one more reward.

conditions.notes: Save your HP for the fights that matter.

choices.label: Tinker with your cards (upgrade or modify a card)
choices.notes: Card modification in Act 3 can push a good deck to great. Upgrade your most important card.

conditions.notes: Every upgrade is amplified in a lean deck.

choices.label: Tinker with your relics (modify or enhance a relic)
choices.notes: Relic modification is rare and can be powerful. Worth trying if your relics could use a boost.

choices.label: Ask the tinker for gold
choices.notes: Gold in Act 3 is only useful if there's still a shop ahead. Otherwise, take the card or relic upgrade.

conditions.notes: Only if a shop is still on your path.
Late in Act 3, gold is usually less useful than direct power.

choices.label: Learn about upcoming enemies (gain combat intelligence)
choices.notes: Knowing enemy patterns helps you play optimally. More useful if you're unfamiliar with Act 3 encounters.

choices.label: Request a battle blessing (gain a permanent combat buff)
choices.notes: Permanent buffs heading into the final boss are extremely valuable. Usually the best pick.

conditions.notes: A combat buff right before the boss is exactly what you want.

choices.label: 골드 획득
choices.notes: A smaller reward but still useful. Good if the buff option doesn't appeal.

conditions.notes: The gold can fund final shop purchases.
